summary: Markets consolidated Saturday's strong May 1 close, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq both notching fresh all-time highs to cap April's best monthly gain since 2020—a 10% surge driven by Magnificent 7 earnings. The Federal Reserve's April 29 decision to hold rates at 3.50–3.75% came with the highest dissent count since 1992, as four officials split over language signaling future easing. Chair Powell, who announced he will stay on as a governor after his chairmanship ends May 15, warned that inflation remains elevated, citing a 21% spike in gasoline and ongoing Middle East tensions. Bitcoin ETFs posted $1.97 billion in April inflows, the strongest month of 2026, as BTC reclaimed $78,000 despite a late-week outflow streak. Oil cooled marginally below $104, offering brief relief, while investors await the CLARITY Act and May earnings from Palantir, AMD, and Disney.

signals.title: Fed holds rates with historic 4-dissent split, flags sticky inflation
signals.desc: The FOMC held rates steady but recorded the most dissents since 1992, with Powell noting elevated inflation from energy and Middle East uncertainty—casting doubt on near-term cuts.

signals.title: Bitcoin ETFs log strongest monthly inflow of 2026 at $1.97B
signals.desc: U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs pulled $1.97 billion in April, nearly double March's total, as BlackRock's IBIT dominated flows and BTC reclaimed the $78,000 level.

signals.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit records after 10% April rally on tech earnings
signals.desc: Both indexes closed May 1 at all-time highs following strong results from Apple, Alphabet, and Eli Lilly, marking the best monthly performance since April 2020.

signals.title: Meta plunges 10% on raised capex guidance to $145B
signals.desc: Meta shares tumbled despite beating Q1 estimates, as the company hiked full-year AI spending guidance and user growth disappointed, stoking fears of low ROI.

signals.title: Oil retreats from $119 peak as Iran talks remain stalled
signals.desc: Brent crude eased below $104 from a near four-year high after reports Iran submitted a new proposal, though Trump rejected reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

verdict: Risk appetite is intact post-April's rally, but Fed hawkishness and oil volatility leave the path to new highs fragile until earnings and macro data clarify demand strength.
